mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a three-month window, it triggers a recessionary fear premium that forces equity deleveraging and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal exhibits no predictive power after controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of housing starts and retail sales signals a synchronized demand shock that erodes corporate revenue growth expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.02 compared to a single-proxy baseline, or forward returns remain positive when both series decline simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of sustained industrial production growth above 105, supply chain normalization expands profit margins, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO exceeds 105 for three consecutive months, or the signal loses statistical significance after adjusting for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in real GDP growth accelerates corporate earnings revisions, which immediately lifts equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling Spearman correlation between GDPC1 revisions and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.10 across all available data windows, indicating no causal link.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5 percent, the cost of capital exceeds the sustainable growth rate of corporate earnings, triggering a valuation multiple compression that yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand or remain stable despite the rate threshold breach, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference between periods above and below 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration of nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor demand expansion, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over PAYEMS alone in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery environment where housing starts rebound by more than 10 percent from cycle lows, construction-related earnings revisions accelerate equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain flat or negative when housing starts surge, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in walk-forward testing across three distinct recovery episodes.
